text.title: The Mom Test Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding mom-test. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every question in the script MUST be about the prospect's life, not the interviewer's idea. Forbidden openers include 'Would you use', 'Do you think', 'Would you pay'.
rule.rationale: Hypotheticals about the idea produce compliments. Talking about their life produces facts.

rule.statement: All exploratory questions MUST be past-tense ('Tell me about the last time') or current-state ('Walk me through how you do X today'). Future-hypothetical questions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Past behavior predicts; future intentions are wishful thinking shaped by social desirability.

rule.statement: When pain is suspected the script MUST include one current-spend question: 'What are you doing about this today?' or 'What does this cost you this month?'
rule.rationale: If no current spend, the pain is not painful enough to attract money — single highest-signal question.

rule.statement: Post-call classification MUST score commitment on the ladder: time (next meeting), reputation (referral), money (deposit / current spend). Verbal enthusiasm without ladder commitment classifies as compliment.
rule.rationale: Compliments feel good but predict nothing; commitments predict adoption.

rule.statement: Interviewer word-share MUST be ≤ 20% measured by token count in the transcript. Above that, the script became a pitch.
rule.rationale: Talking more than 20% turns discovery into selling — guarantees compliments, kills signal.
